words.definition: The pointed top of a mountain; the highest point of something.
The most successful point; the culmination.
The time at which something is most powerful or successful; the highest point in the sky directly overhead.
The point at which someone or something is best, perfect, or most successful.
Apogee: The highest point in the development of something. Perigee: The lowest or closest point.
The most intense, exciting, or important point of something.
The highest point; the top or apex.
The top or highest part of something, especially one forming a point.
A deep or seemingly bottomless chasm.
The lowest point in the fortunes of a person or organization.
Extremely bad; appalling. (Figuratively meaning "bottomless" like an abyss).
To show or declare that someone is not guilty of wrongdoing.
To officially absolve someone from blame for a fault or wrongdoing.
To clear someone of blame or suspicion; to prove to be right or justified.
To persuade someone that an idea or belief is mistaken.
To set or declare someone free from blame, guilt, or responsibility.
A state of temporary disuse or suspension.
The condition or period of spending the winter in a dormant state.
Having normal physical functions suspended or slowed down for a period of time; temporarily inactive.
In a state or period of inactivity or dormancy.
Lacking the ability or strength to move; chemically inactive.
The temporary prevention of something from continuing or being in force or effect.
The fact or process of ending or being brought to an end.
Existing but not yet developed or manifest; hidden or concealed.
In a state of deep unconsciousness for a prolonged or indefinite period; extremely lethargic.
Providing supplementary or additional help and support.
Providing necessary support to the primary activities or operation of an organization, institution, industry, or system.
A strong, often prejudiced, supporter of a party, cause, or person.
Preconceived opinion that is not based on reason or actual experience.
To believe in and follow the practices of; to stick to a belief.
A person who is obstinately or unreasonably attached to a belief or faction, especially one who is prejudiced against others.
Having or expressing devotion to and vigorous support for one's country.
Feeling or displaying aggressive or exaggerated patriotism; excessively biased.
Extreme patriotism, especially in the form of aggressive or warlike foreign policy.
Giving or showing firm and constant support or allegiance to a person or institution.
Of or involving preference or partiality; showing favor to one over another.
To publicly recommend or support a particular cause or policy.
To vigorously support or defend a person or cause.
To provide a source of defense or support; to increase the strength of or justification for something.
To support or strengthen; prop up.
To confirm or give support to a statement, theory, or finding.
To adopt or support a cause, belief, or way of life.
To encourage or assist someone.
To encourage or promote the development of something (usually something good).
To declare one's public approval or support of something or someone.
To support, justify, or form the basis for something.
To provide support or a firm basis for something; to secure from underneath.
To sign or give formal consent to a treaty, contract, or agreement, making it officially valid.
To prove or show that something (like a claim or document) is true, genuine, or valid.
To check or prove the validity, accuracy, or acceptability of something.
A person who advocates for a theory, proposal, or project.
A person advocating the restoration to their country of any territory formerly belonging to it.
To shorten a book, movie, speech, or other text without losing the overall sense.
The main or essential part of a matter; the substance of a speech or text.
Pithy: (of language) concise and forcefully expressive. Pith: The essence of something.
Sparing in the use of words; abrupt or short in speech.
Tacit: Understood or implied without being stated aloud. Taciturn: (of a person) reserved or uncommunicative; saying very little.
Using very few words.
A brief summary or general survey of something.
Concise and exact use of words in writing or speech; shortness of time.
A collection of concise but detailed information about a particular subject.
Briefly and clearly expressed.
A connection or series of connections linking two or more things.
Commonplace or dull; matter-of-fact or unimaginative.
Ordinary; not unusual, interesting, or original.
Lacking inspiration or excitement; dull.
Lacking originality; derivative or copied.
Imitative of the work of someone else, usually seen as lacking originality.
(Of a phrase or idea) lacking significance through having been overused; unoriginal.
A phrase or opinion that is overused and betrays a lack of original thought.
Lacking interest or excitement; everyday and ordinary.
Of only ordinary or moderate quality; barely adequate.
Ordinary or everyday, especially when mundane.
Commonplace or dull.
Lacking vigor or interest; boring.
A trite and unoriginal idea or remark, typically intended to soothe or placate.
Offering nothing that is stimulating or challenging; flat or dull.
Lacking in vitality, force, or conviction; uninspired.
(Of a remark, opinion, or idea) overused and consequently of little import.
So lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring.
The masses; the common people.
Lacking excitement or variety; dull; monotonous.
A remark or statement, especially one with moral content, that has been used too often to be interesting.
A statement that is obviously true and says nothing new or interesting.
Criticism or censure.
Characterized by malice; intending to do harm.
To regard with extreme repugnance or aversion.
To look down on with contempt or aversion.
To speak harmful untruths about; speak evil of.
To feel disgust or intense aversion for.
To feel abhorrence of; hate.
A natural, basic, or habitual repugnance; aversion.
Active hostility or opposition.
Anything greatly disliked or abhorred.
A strong feeling of dislike, opposition, or repugnance.
A strong feeling of repugnance, distaste, or dislike.
The act of cursing or denouncing; something strongly detested.
Strong distaste, aversion, or objection; antipathy.
Clever or cunning devices or expedients, especially as used to trick or deceive others.
To fool or cheat someone.
The practice of taking someone else's work or ideas and passing them off as one's own.
To use deception to deprive someone of money or possessions.
Sly or cunning intelligence.
Having or showing skill and sensitivity in dealing with others.
The practice of deception.
Having or showing skill in achieving one's ends by deceit or evasion.
Deceit used in order to achieve one's goal.
A person falsely claiming to have a special knowledge or skill; a fraud.
A person who dishonestly claims to have special knowledge in some field, typically medicine.
Made by chemical synthesis to imitate a natural product; fake or not genuine.
Made or used as a substitute, typically an inferior one, for something else.
A person who deceives others, especially in order to trick them out of their money.
To cheat or swindle someone out of something.
To fool or deceive someone.
A person who pretends to be someone else in order to deceive others.
To steal or misappropriate money placed in one's trust.
Deliberately cause someone to believe something that is not true.
To persuade someone to do or believe something by use of deception.
Artful: Cleverly deceitful or cunning. Stultify: To cause someone to appear foolish or absurd.
Not genuine; fraudulent.
To charm or enchant someone, typically in a deceptive way.
Not genuine; sham.
To take something for one's own use, typically without the owner's permission.
To conceal one's true motives, feelings, or beliefs.
To deceive or trick someone.
To defraud, overcharge, or swindle someone.
To obtain or withhold money from someone by deceit; to cheat.
The use of trickery to achieve a political, financial, or legal purpose.
A person who is dishonest or a criminal.
To trick or deceive; to obtain by deception.
A proverb or short statement expressing a general truth.
A pithy observation that contains a general truth.
A short pithy saying in general use, stating a general truth or piece of advice.
A phrase established by usage whose meaning is not deducible from the individual words.
A short, well-known expression that generally contains advice or wisdom.
A synonym for a proverb or maxim.
Repeat or copy out a group of words from a text or speech.
A short, pithy statement expressing a general truth or rule of conduct.
A statement that is obviously true and says nothing new or interesting.
An old saying or commonly repeated phrase.
A short sentence or phrase chosen to encapsulate beliefs.
A statement or proposition which is regarded as being established, accepted, or self-evidently true.
To reprimand someone severely.
The act of speaking about someone in a negative or belittling way.
The action of unfairly criticizing someone or something.
Strong public criticism or verbal abuse.
A lengthy and aggressive speech.
A long, angry speech of criticism or accusation.
Public condemnation of someone or something.
A forceful and bitter verbal attack against someone or something.
Bitter and abusive language.
To find fault with someone; scold.
To express severe disapproval of someone or something, typically in a formal statement.
To express complete disapproval of, typically in public.
Abase: To behave in a way that belittles or degrades someone. Debase: To reduce in quality or value.
To warn or reprimand someone firmly.
To express sharp disapproval or criticism of someone because of their behavior.
To scold or rebuke.
Insulting, abusive, or highly critical language.
Severe criticism; the act of censuring or scolding harshly.
Carp (to complain continually) or Jeer (to make rude/mocking remarks).
A sternly critical remark or instruction.
To insult someone by treating or speaking of them without proper respect or attention.
A strong verbal or written attack on someone or something.
Rejection of a proposal or idea.
Censure; reproof; the act of expressing disapproval.
To criticize severely (often used for reviews of movies or books).
To rebuke or reprimand severely.
To criticize someone or something harshly.
To scold or criticize someone angrily.
To remonstrate with or rebuke someone angrily.
To censure or criticize severely.
To reprimand or censure someone.
The making of false and defamatory statements about someone in order to damage their reputation.
An expression of vehement protest or denunciation.
Deserving or causing public disgrace or shame.
To complain or protest strongly and persistently about someone or something.
General or widespread hatred or disgust directed toward someone as a result of their actions.
Harsh criticism or censure.
The action or crime of making a false spoken statement damaging to a person's reputation.
To speak or write about in an abusively disparaging manner.
Loss of reputation or respect as the result of a dishonorable action.
Making or spreading scandalous claims about someone with the intention of damaging their reputation.
A painful feeling of humiliation or distress caused by the consciousness of wrong or foolish behavior.
To give a servile display of exaggerated flattery or affection, typically in order to gain favor.
To gently and persistently persuade someone to do something.
To persuade someone to do something by sustained coaxing or flattery.
To use flattery or coaxing in order to persuade someone to do something or give one something.
Obsequious flattery; excessive admiration or praise.
A flattering or pleasing statement or action used to persuade someone gently to do something.
Exaggerated or false praise.
Obedient or attentive to an excessive or servile degree.
Obsequious behavior toward someone important in order to gain advantage.
A person who behaves in a highly obsequious manner.
A person who behaves obsequiously to someone important.
Talk that aims to charm, pleasantly flatter, or persuade.
A state of physical or emotional balance.
Balance of forces or interests.
Mental calmness, composure, and evenness of temper, especially in a difficult situation.
A period or state of inactivity or equilibrium.
Composure or coolness, sometimes excessive, as shown in danger or under trying circumstances.
The state of being calm, peaceful, and untroubled.
Calm, peaceful, and untroubled; tranquil.
Free from disturbance; calm.
A state of rest, sleep, or tranquility.
Not easily upset or excited; calm and peaceful.
A person, especially a man, who behaves without moral principles or a sense of responsibility.
Lax in morals; licentious.
Squandering of money, energy, or resources; overindulgence in sensual pleasures.
Excessive indulgence in sensual pleasures.
Having or characterized by many transient sexual relationships.
Morally corrupt; wicked.
(Of a cruel or violent action) deliberate and unprovoked; sexually immodest or promiscuous.
Promiscuous and unprincipled in sexual matters.
Fond of giving people orders; domineering.
Not qualified or diminished in any way; total.
Enforcing strict obedience to authority, especially that of the government, at the expense of personal freedom.
Unjustly inflicting hardship and constraint, especially on a minority or other subordinate group.
Relating to a ruler who has absolute power.
Of or typical of a ruler with total power.
Relating to or using force or threats.
Exercising power in a cruel or arbitrary way.
Of or typical of a despot; tyrannical.
Having or showing great authority.
Assuming power or authority without justification; arrogant and domineering.
An advocate or follower of fascism; highly authoritarian or intolerant.
Ruling or dominant in a political or social context.
Relating to a system of government that is centralized and dictatorial.
Inclined to lay down principles as incontrovertibly true.
A ruler with total power over a country.
A person who is obstinately or unreasonably attached to a belief, opinion, or faction.
An independent person or body officially appointed to settle a dispute.
The use of an arbitrator to settle a dispute.
The establishment or starting point of an institution or activity.
A beginning or start.
The beginning of something, especially something unpleasant.
The start or beginning of something.
The record of descent of an animal or person.
The origin or mode of formation of something.
The state of being born or coming into existence.
In an initial stage; beginning to happen or develop.
Involving or limited to basic principles; immature or undeveloped.
In a rudimentary stage with potential for further development.
Beginning to develop or show signs of future potential.
A person or thing that comes before another of the same kind.
Just begun and so not fully formed or developed.
A person who is new to or inexperienced at a particular activity.
A person who engages in a pursuit on an unpaid basis.
A person who cultivates an area of interest without real commitment or knowledge.
A person or organization that is immature, inexperienced, or underdeveloped.
A young person making their first appearance in society.
A person without professional or specialized knowledge in a particular subject.
Relating to, denoting, or preserving the character of an early stage in evolutionary or historical development.
In its original condition; unspoiled.
Existing at or from the beginning of time; primeval.
The position of a student or trainee who works in an organization.
A person who is learning a trade from a skilled employer.
A person new to or inexperienced in a field or situation.
A person of obscure origin who has gained wealth, influence, or celebrity.
Begin to grow or increase rapidly; flourish.
A young child who is just beginning to walk.
The state or period of early childhood or babyhood.
The period or state of being a novice.
A person who has converted from one opinion, religion, or party to another.
A person who is new to a subject, skill, or belief.
Green with grass; (figuratively) inexperienced.
A beginner or novice.
Inexperienced and immature.
Cause or give rise to a feeling, situation, or condition.
Sparing or economical with regard to money or food.
A person who hoards wealth and spends as little money as possible.
Unwilling to give or spend; ungenerous.
Unwilling to spend money or use resources; stingy or frugal.
Unwilling to give or share things, especially money; not generous.
Unwilling to spend money.
Not generous; stingy.
Extremely poor; poverty-stricken, or parsimonious.
Management and conservation of resources.
Having or showing shrewdness and good judgment, especially in money or business matters.
To use resources economically.
Not willing to spend or give much money; stingy.
Not willing to spend or give much money; stingy.
Acting with or showing care and thought for the future.
Using money and other resources carefully and not wastefully.
Making or indicating timely preparation for the future.
A supply of a commodity not needed for immediate use but available if required.
Lacking restraint in spending money or using resources.
Spending money or resources freely and recklessly; wastefully extravagant.
Waste something, especially money or time, in a reckless and foolish manner.
A wasteful or good-for-nothing person.
Great wealth or luxuriousness.
Moderate; economical.
Recklessly extravagant or wasteful in the use of resources.
A large or excessive amount of something.
The state of having a great deal of money; wealth.
The state or feeling, often pleasant, of tiredness or inertia.
Slow-moving or inactive.
Lacking in vitality, force, or conviction.
Avoidance of activity or exertion; laziness.
Lazy.
Avoiding work; lazy.
Mentally or physically inactive; lethargic.
Affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic.
Lacking care or attention to duty; negligent.
Sleepy and lethargic; half asleep.
Sleepy; drowsy.
Displaying or having a disinclination for physical exertion or effort.
Lacking enthusiasm and determination; carelessly lazy.
Temporarily inactive.
Not taut or held tightly in position; loose.
Careless and unsystematic; excessively casual.
Failing to take proper care in doing something.
Showing a reckless lack of care or attention.
In a very poor condition as a result of disuse and neglect.
Done too hurriedly and carelessly.
Delaying or delayed beyond the right or expected time; late.
Be deprived of a loved one through a profound absence, especially due to the loved one's death.
A corpse.
A person's death.
Feeling or expressing great sorrow or distress.
A lament for the dead, especially one forming part of a funeral rite.
A passionate expression of grief or sorrow.
Disturbing and horrifying because of involvement with or depiction of death and injury.
At the point of death.
A large vault, typically an underground one, for burying the dead.
Looking or sounding sad and dismal.
Characterized by, expressive of, or causing sorrow or misery.
Dark or dull in color or tone; gloomy.
A feeling of pensive sadness, typically with no obvious cause.
Happening or carried on according to chance rather than design or inherent nature.
An ornament or small piece of jewelry thought to give protection against evil, danger, or disease.
Conducive to success; favorable.
Happening by accident or chance rather than design.
Destiny; fate.
Of or like a portent; done in a pompously or overly solemn manner so as to impress.
Win or regain favor.
A sign or warning that something, especially something momentous or calamitous, is likely to happen.
Occurring at a favorable time; opportune.
The occurrence and development of events by chance in a happy or beneficial way.
An object, typically an inscribed ring or stone, that is thought to have magic powers and to bring good luck.
A change of circumstances or fortune, typically one that is unwelcome or unpleasant.
A thing belonging or appropriate to a period other than that in which it exists.
Old-fashioned or outdated.
Precede in time; come before something in date.
Coming before in time; earlier.
Very old or old-fashioned.
An indefinite and very long period of time.
A period of time in history or a person's life, typically one marked by notable events or particular characteristics.
Following a twisting or spiral course.
Move swiftly and in an uncontrolled way in a specified direction.
Longer than the most direct way.
Find a way around an obstacle.
A complicated irregular network of passages or paths in which it is difficult to find one's way.
A network of paths and hedges designed as a puzzle through which one has to find a way.
Follow a winding course.
Of or like a serpent or snake; winding and twisting.
Having many curves and turns.
Bent or twisted out of shape or out of place.
Neither parallel nor at a right angle to a specified or implied line; slanting.
With an attitude or look of suspicion or disapproval.
Leave the main subject temporarily in speech or writing.
Having a smoothly rising and falling form or outline.
Written or spoken communication or debate. (May be written incorrectly for 'Discursive').
Not in a straight or level position.
Away from the appropriate, planned, or expected course; amiss.
Relating to or adapted for walking.
Traveling from place to place.
Traveling from place to place, in particular working or based in various places for relatively short periods.
A journey, especially a long or meandering one.
A temporary stay.
The action of intruding.
A long or roundabout route that is taken to avoid something or to visit somewhere along the way.
A long walk, especially in the country or wilderness.
Wander at random.
Walk in a leisurely way.
Solemnly renounce a belief, cause, or claim.
Renounce one's throne.
Failing to produce the intended result.
Depart suddenly or secretly.
Say that one no longer holds an opinion or belief.
The cancellation of a debt, charge, or penalty.
Revoke, cancel, or repeal a law, order, or agreement.
Go back on a promise, undertaking, or contract.
Directed or moving backward.
Draw or be drawn back or back in.
Revoke or annul a law or congressional act.
Abolish, invalidate, or reverse a previous system, decision, situation, etc.
Revoke an order.
Bring a fact, event, or situation back into one's mind; officially order to return.
Withdraw from enemy forces as a result of their superior power or after a defeat.
Declare invalid.
Put an end to the validity or operation of a decree, decision, or promise.
Give up power or territory.
Make something bad or unsatisfactory better.
Pacify or placate someone by acceding to their demands.
Make an unpleasant feeling less intense.
Make less severe, serious, or painful.
Appease the anger or anxiety of someone.
Make someone less angry or hostile.
Win or regain the favor of a god, spirit, or person by doing something that pleases them.
Satisfy a desire or an appetite to the full.
Quench or satisfy one's thirst.
Gently calm a person or their feelings.
Make suffering, deficiency, or a problem less severe.
Not easily upset or excited.
Pleasing to the ear.
Of, producing, or having a pleasant tune; tuneful.
Imposingly deep and full.
Sweet and soothing.
Sweet or musical; pleasant to hear.
Tuneful; not discordant.
The quality of being open and honest in expression; frankness.
Leaving no doubt; unambiguous.
Truthful and straightforward; frank.
A feudal tenant's or vassal's sworn loyalty to a lord.
The quality of being open, honest, and direct in speech or writing.
Impossible to doubt; unquestionable.
The quality of having strong moral principles; honesty and decency.
The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles.
Conforming to the law or to rules.
Poor; needy.
Made poor.
Having little or no money.
Extremely poor; poverty-stricken.
Extreme poverty; destitution.
Having no money; very poor.
A very poor person.
Without the basic necessities of life.
A state in which things that are essential for human well-being such as food and warmth are scarce or lacking.
Lacking in quantity or quality.
Thinly dispersed or scattered.
The amount by which something, especially a sum of money, is too small.
A scarcity or lack of something.
The presence of something only in small or insufficient quantities or amounts.
Extreme scarcity of food.
Thinly dispersed or scattered.
Moderate; economical.
A stingy or ungenerous person.
Slow to act; intended to cause delay.
A person who makes slow progress and falls behind others.
Stand or wait around idly or without apparent purpose.
Lasting for a long time or slow to end.
Delay or postpone action; put off doing something.
Stay longer than intended; delay leaving a place.
Waste time through aimless wandering or indecision.
Delaying or delayed beyond the right or expected time; late.
Annoyingly slow.
Resembling a snail, especially in being very slow.
Lie, sit, or stand in a relaxed or lazy way.
Delayed or held back in progress or development.
